data.title: STOCKS | ARK Funds Buy 3.29 Million Shares of SpaceX and Cut AMD Stake
data.description:
ARK Invest disclosed its trading records for June 12, 2026, showing a notable shift in its positioning. According to Jin10, several ARK exchange-traded funds collectively bought 3,291,184 shares of SpaceX stock, with total transactions valued at $444,309,840, indicating increased interest in the space technology sector.

At the same time, ARK significantly reduced its holdings in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). Through three ETFs—ARKQ, ARKW, and ARKX—the firm sold a combined 80,536 AMD shares, with transactions totaling $39,337,809, continuing its trend of trimming the position.

data.title: World Cup Day Two: USA Win 4-1 as Canada Claim Historic Draw
data.description:
The United States made a flying start on Matchday 2 of the 2026 World Cup with a commanding 4-1 win over Paraguay, while Canada fought back to earn a historic draw against Bosnia and Herzegovina.
In Group B, Canada faced Bosnia and Herzegovina in a tense contest. Bosnia and Herzegovina took the lead in the 21st minute when Jovo Lukić headed home from a corner, putting Canada under early pressure.
However, Canada responded after the break through substitute Cyle Larin, who made an instant impact by scoring just 121 seconds after coming on. His goal brought Canada level at 1-1 and secured a memorable result for the co-host.
The draw marked Canada’s first-ever point at a FIFA World Cup, coming 40 years after their tournament debut. It also ended a run of defeats across their previous World Cup matches.
In Group D, the United States impressed with a strong attacking display against Paraguay. The co-host went ahead in the seventh minute when a Weston McKennie pass deflected off Damian Bobadilla and into the net for an own goal.
Folarin Balogun then struck twice before half-time to give the USA a 3-0 lead at the break. Paraguay pulled one back in the second half through Mauricio, but Giovanni Reyna sealed the win in stoppage time to complete a 4-1 victory.
It was a landmark result for the United States, who scored four goals in a World Cup match for the first time. The win also matched their biggest margin of victory in the competition, equalling their three-goal wins over Belgium and Paraguay 96 years ago.
Upcoming Matches (all times local)
Tomorrow’s matches are scheduled for June 13:
12:00 — Qatar v Switzerland, Group B18:00 — Brazil v Morocco, Group C21:00 — Haiti v Scotland, Group C21:00 — Australia v Türkiye, Group D

data.title: Israel Says It Will Need to Act Against Hezbollah Over Ceasefire Violations
data.description:
Israel’s military said it will have to take action against Hezbollah, citing violations of a ceasefire agreement.

According to Odaily, the military said the action would be necessary because Hezbollah had breached the ceasefire terms.

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs See $4.95 Million Net Outflow, Led by BlackRock’s ETHA
data.description:
Ethereum spot ETFs recorded a total net outflow of $4.9452 million as of yesterday, based on SoSoValue data.

According to NS3.AI, BlackRock’s ETHA posted the largest single-day net outflow among the products, totaling $4.53 million.

data.title: Anthropic pre-IPO shares slide after US orders shutdown of Fable 5 and Mythos 5
data.description: Anthropic’s pre-IPO market sold off after the US government ordered the company to shut down access to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. According to CoinDesk, the Anthropic perpetual on Hyperliquid fell 3.7% on Saturday to about $1,627 after trading above $1,800 following the Fable 5 launch; open interest is about $8.6 million. Anthropic said it received the directive at 5:21 p.m. ET on Friday, citing national security authorities, and executed a full shutdown after being told of a method to jailbreak Fable 5. The company disputed the evidence and said it will share more details within 24 hours.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Perpetual on Hyperliquid Falls 3.7% After U.S. Orders Access Shutdown for Fable 5 and Mythos 5
data.description:
The U.S. government ordered Anthropic to shut down access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and the Anthropic perpetual on Hyperliquid fell about 3.7%.

According to NS3.AI, Anthropic challenged the directive, saying the government provided only verbal evidence of a narrow vulnerability that was previously known.

Anthropic said the issue cited by the government was limited in scope and not newly discovered.

data.title: Lookonchain: SIREN Whale Realizes Over 7.5 Million USDT and Continues Selling
data.description:
A whale has realized more than 7.5 million USDT by selling SIREN, according to on-chain monitoring data from Lookonchain.

According to ChainCatcher, the whale still holds 595.7 million SIREN, and the sell-off is continuing.

data.title: Geely to Streamline Structure, Focus Resources on Hong Kong-Listed Arm
data.description: China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group will streamline its structure by shutting down or merging some entities and will concentrate resources around its Hong Kong-listed arm, Chairman Li Shufu said. According to Bloomberg, the move is aimed at improving governance.

data.title: SpaceX Discloses 18,712 Bitcoin Holdings in SEC S-1 Filing
data.description:
SpaceX disclosed that it holds 18,712 Bitcoin worth about $1.18 billion, according to information included in its S-1 IPO filing submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. According to NS3.AI, the disclosure surfaced through the S-1 filing materials.

The report said Bitcointreasuries data indicates SpaceX’s average purchase cost was approximately $35,000 per Bitcoin.

data.title: Italian Deputy PM Tajani Criticizes ECB Interest-Rate Hike
data.description: Italian Deputy Prime Minister Antonio Tajani criticized the European Central Bank’s interest-rate hike. In comments reported by Bloomberg, Tajani said the move “doesn’t help anyone.”

data.title: SpaceX Equity Token on Solana-Based PreStocks Trades at 26.6% Discount
data.description:
A SpaceX equity token listed on PreStocks, a Solana-based pre-IPO tokenization product, is trading at a notable discount, with the website showing a price of about $118.17, or 26.6% below the publicly traded SPCX share price. According to PANews, the discount is suspected to be linked to a 180-day lock-up period on PreStocks, during which the token cannot be redeemed for real shares.

The report said holders currently have two options: sell immediately at a discount in a market with relatively limited liquidity, or wait 180 days to redeem the token for real shares at full value.

data.title: SIREN Drops Over 50% After Controller Sells About 17 Million Tokens On-Chain
data.description:
SIREN fell more than 50% after a controller sold about 17 million siren-2:native tokens on-chain through multiple addresses over the past 2 hours.

According to Foresight News, monitoring data from Yu Jin said the sell-off contributed to SIREN sliding from $0.47 to $0.23.

The report said SIREN is heavily controlled by a single operator, who is estimated to hold at least 94% of the total supply of 680 million tokens.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 600 USDT with a 0.74% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jun 13, 2026, 04:58 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 600 USDT and is now trading at 599.5 USDT, with a narrowed 0.74% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Analyst Jukan Says Countries’ Assumptions About Importing U.S. Large Language Models Have Been Challenged
data.description: Citrini analyst Jukan said in a post on X that the belief that countries can directly import and use large language models from the United States has been challenged. According to Odaily, Jukan said this is because such an arrangement would mean the United States could cut off access at any time.

data.title: Standard Chartered’s Kendrick Says Bitcoin Has Bottomed Near $59,000
data.description:
Standard Chartered analyst Geoffrey Kendrick said Bitcoin has bottomed near $59,000. According to NS3.AI, Kendrick said this level represents a 53% decline from the previous all-time high of $126,000.

Kendrick said he will monitor Strategy buying and U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF inflows this week.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Accused of Appeasing Trump, Fars News Reports
data.description:
Iranian media outlet Fars News reported that Iran’s foreign minister has been accused of harming Iran’s negotiating position by aligning with U.S. President Donald Trump’s approach.

According to Odaily, the report did not provide further details on the accusations.

data.title: U.S. Tops World Cup Group D Opener as Predict.fun Odds Rise to 59%
data.description:
The United States beat Paraguay 4-1 in the first round of World Cup Group D matches.

According to Odaily, monitoring of the predict.fun event “World Cup Group D First Place” showed the probability of the United States finishing first in Group D rose to 59%, up 21 percentage points over the past 24 hours.

Odaily reported that Paraguay’s probability of finishing first in Group D fell to 3%.

data.title: Zcash Founder Says Mythos Audit Found No Additional Critical Flaws in Zcash Protocol
data.description:
Zcash founder Zooko Wilcox said a security audit of Zcash conducted using Mythos did not identify additional critical vulnerabilities in the Zcash protocol.

According to ChainCatcher, Wilcox said the audit was carried out at the request of Shielded Labs, with Anthropic providing assistance aimed at protecting Zcash users.

Wilcox added that Shielded Labs and other teams are continuing work to strengthen security, and that further updates will be released.

data.title: Pudgy Penguins Halts Pudgy Party Development to Focus on Browser Game Pudgy World
data.description:
Pudgy Penguins has announced it will stop developing its Web3 game Pudgy Party and will shift its focus to the free, browser-based game Pudgy World. According to PANews, the project will concentrate resources on Pudgy World going forward.

In August last year, the NFT brand launched Pudgy Party, a Web3 mobile game developed in partnership with Mythical Games. The game featured fast-paced mini-games, customization, and digital collectibles.

In March this year, Pudgy Penguins launched Pudgy World as a free game that runs in a web browser.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Says US Killed Tren De Aragua Leader Niño Guerrero in Strike
data.description: The US killed Tren De Aragua drug cartel leader Niño Guerrero in a strike coordinated with Venezuela, US President Donald Trump said. According to Bloomberg, Trump described the operation as coordinated with Venezuela.
